title: Slow-cooker sausage casserole
subtitle: Pork sausages braised in a smoky tomato sauce
excerpt: Pork sausages browned and slow-cooked with tinned tomatoes, onion, pepper, smoked paprika, and Worcestershire sauce for six hours. The sausages take on the sauce and the sauce takes on the sausage fat. A British weeknight dinner that requires almost nothing.
sourceNotes: Sausage casserole is a standard of British home cooking, appearing in cookbooks from the post-war period as a practical way to extend a small number of sausages into a complete family meal. The slow cooker version became popular because it requires no supervision after the initial browning. The smoked paprika is a modern addition, not traditional, but it has become standard in most home cook versions.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well. The sausages hold their shape but become very tender on reheating.

content.text: Brown the sausages before they go in. A slow cooker cannot colour them — they will cook through but remain pale and lack the caramelised exterior that contributes to both flavour and texture. Five minutes in a hot pan, turning once, is enough.

Brown the {{sausages-pork}} in a dry pan over medium heat, turning until coloured on all sides — about 5 minutes. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{onion}}, {{garlic}}, {{carrot}}, {{tinned-tomatoes}}, {{tomato-puree}}, {{smoked-paprika}}, {{worcestershire-sauce}}, {{stock-chicken}}, {{bay-leaves}}, {{thyme-fresh}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{black-pepper}}. Stir around the sausages. Cook on low for 6 hours. Remove the {{bay-leaves}} and {{thyme-fresh}} sprigs. Taste and adjust seasoning.

items.cause: Tinned tomatoes released a lot of water
items.fix: Remove the lid for the last 30 minutes and cook on high to reduce. A teaspoon of cornflour mixed with cold water and stirred in also thickens quickly

items.cause: Not browned before the slow cooker
items.fix: Always brown sausages first. The caramelised exterior is part of the finished texture. Raw sausages that go in pale come out soft and lacking flavour
The flavour of a sausage casserole depends heavily on the sausages. Higher-pork-content sausages (80% and above) produce a richer result than cheap brands

content.text: Sausage casserole is a British family dinner that works across all ages. The slow cooker version is built in ten minutes in the morning and needs nothing more before serving. Serve over mashed potato or with crusty bread. Any leftovers reheat well the next day.
